@@ -0,0 +1,60 @@
+# AOG Worker Task: aefa4e4f
+
+## Task
+
+Create file /root/minecraftlab/webui/src/lib/addon-deps.ts - a dependency resolver for Minecraft Bedrock addons.
+
+Available imports:
+```typescript
+import { getAddonFiles, getAddonsByIds, type AddonFile, type AddonSummary } from "./curseforge";
+```
+
+CurseForge files have dependencies: `{ modId: number; relationType: number }[]`
+relationType: 1=EmbeddedLibrary, 2=OptionalDependency, 3=RequiredDependency, 5=Incompatible
+
+Implement and export:
+```typescript
+export interface DependencyInfo {
+  modId: number;
+  name: string;
+  fileId: number;
+  fileName: string;
+  downloadUrl: string | null;
+  relationType: number;
+  isInstalled: boolean;
+}
+export interface DependencyResult {
+  required: DependencyInfo[];
+  optional: DependencyInfo[];
+  incompatible: DependencyInfo[];
+  errors: string[];
+}
+export async function resolveDependencies(
+  dependencies: { modId: number; relationType: number }[],
+  installedModIds: number[]
+): Promise<DependencyResult>
+```
+
+Logic:
+1. Group deps by relationType (3=required, 2=optional, 5=incompatible)
+2. Mark isInstalled if modId is in installedModIds
+3. Batch-fetch addon details via getAddonsByIds for required+optional deps
+4. For each uninstalled dep, get latest file via getAddonFiles(modId) and pick files[0]
+5. Build DependencyInfo with name from addon, fileId/fileName/downloadUrl from file
+6. Never throw - populate errors[] array on failure
+7. Export all interfaces and function. No `any` types. Proper TypeScript.
+
+## Expected Output
+
+When you complete the task:
+1. Make all code changes in the working directory
+2. Ensure the code compiles and tests pass
+3. Do NOT commit changes — the orchestrator handles git operations
+4. Output format: structured text with code changes
+
+## Constraints
+
+- This is an isolated worktree — changes here do not affect the main branch
+- Do NOT modify files in `.aog/`, `.worktrees/`, or `.git/`
+- Focus on the task described above
+- Be thorough but concise
